title: [TASK] Set up Frontend application with React, TanStack Router, TanStack Query, oRPC client, and i18n

conversation_log.summary: Task is architecturally sound overall but contains several inaccuracies that must be corrected before implementation: (1) all references to pnpm must be changed to npm — the monorepo uses npm workspaces per architecture.md and ADR-001; (2) tsconfig.json must extend @cv-tool/tsconfig, not define strict:true independently; (3) shared package must be pinned to packages/contracts/ (@cv-tool/contracts), removing 'packages/shared' as an alternative; (4) i18n library constrained to react-i18next; (5) bundler confirmed as Vite; (6) oRPC client must read its base URL from an environment variable; (7) pnpm-workspace.yaml should not appear in files-likely-to-change.

Four acceptance criteria require revision before tests can be written unambiguously: (1) AC #4 is untestable as written — it checks for 'strict: true' in compilerOptions directly, which is architecturally non-compliant per the Architect's review and architecture.md §Key Patterns #1; the criterion must instead require the tsconfig.json to extend @cv-tool/tsconfig/react.json and must not set strict flags itself. (2) AC #2 uses 'a documented port' without specifying where it is documented, making the criterion ambiguous and non-binary. (3) AC #8 omits the Architect-required constraint that the oRPC client base URL must come from VITE_API_URL env var and that apps/frontend/.env.example must exist documenting it — without this, the criterion cannot detect a hardcoded URL. (4) AC #12 still references 'pnpm build' (Architect required npm) and uses wrong Turborepo filter flag 'frontend' instead of '@cv-tool/frontend'. ACs #1, #3, #5, #6, #7, #9, #10, #11, #13 are testable as written.

Revised four acceptance criteria in response to Tester rejection (round 1). Changes: (1) AC #2 — replaced vague 'documented port' with specific requirement: port declared in apps/frontend/.env.example (e.g. VITE_PORT=5173) and noted in apps/frontend/README.md. (2) AC #4 — replaced 'strict: true in compilerOptions' with requirement that tsconfig.json extends @cv-tool/tsconfig/react.json, does NOT declare strict/noUncheckedIndexedAccess/exactOptionalPropertyTypes itself, and that tsc --showConfig confirms all three flags resolve to true. (3) AC #8 — added VITE_API_URL env-var constraint: client must read base URL from VITE_API_URL (no hardcoding), apps/frontend/.env.example must exist and document VITE_API_URL, app must not crash if VITE_API_URL is unset or points to non-running host. (4) AC #12 — replaced 'pnpm build' with 'npm run build', corrected Turborepo filter to '--filter=@cv-tool/frontend', and added requirement that a dist/ output directory is produced. Also updated: AC #9 — pinned shared package to @cv-tool/contracts exclusively (removed packages/shared hedge); AC #10 and #11 — explicitly named react-i18next; Files Likely to Change — removed pnpm-workspace.yaml, added apps/frontend/.env.example and apps/frontend/README.md, replaced packages/shared with packages/contracts/. Previous Architect and Tester approvals are invalidated; restarting sign-off process from Architect.

All 13 acceptance criteria are now fully aligned with architecture.md. Verified against: §Structure (workspace layout and package names), §Naming Conventions (@cv-tool/frontend, @cv-tool/contracts), §Key Patterns #1 (tsconfig extends @cv-tool/tsconfig, no local strict overrides), #3 (shared contracts via @cv-tool/contracts), #4 (Turborepo pipeline with correct filter syntax), #5 (no cross-app imports), #6 (environment configuration via .env.example for VITE_API_URL and VITE_PORT), and §Tech Stack (react-i18next, TanStack Router with codegen, TanStack Query, oRPC, Vite, npm workspaces). All corrections from the previous architect review and tester feedback have been incorporated. No architectural changes required.

All 13 acceptance criteria are testable as written (round-2 review). The four previously-rejected criteria have been fully corrected: AC #2 now specifies the exact location of the port (apps/frontend/.env.example and README.md), making an HTTP 200 check deterministic; AC #4 now requires the 'extends' field and absence of strict flags in compilerOptions, with tsc --showConfig as the observable verification; AC #8 now includes the VITE_API_URL env-var constraint, no-hardcoding requirement, and .env.example existence check; AC #12 now references 'npm run build' and '--filter=@cv-tool/frontend' with a dist/ output assertion. All remaining criteria (AC #1, #3, #5, #6, #7, #9, #10, #11, #13) remain binary and observable. No further revision needed.

Implemented full apps/frontend workspace bootstrap. PR #24 opened. All 13 ACs addressed: workspace config with @cv-tool/tsconfig/react.json extension, Vite dev server on VITE_PORT=5173, TanStack Router with file-based codegen (TanStackRouterVite plugin + tsr.config.json), index route at '/' rendering translated content, QueryClientProvider at app root, oRPC client singleton typed against AppRouter reading VITE_API_URL from env, react-i18next with en/fr locales and app.title key, locale toggle button demonstrating multi-locale support, ESLint 9 flat config with typescript-eslint.

risks: oRPC client uses RPCLink (RPC transport) but backend currently only mounts OpenAPIHandler — actual API calls will 404 until backend adds an RPCHandler. The client is correctly typed and initialized; this is a backend concern outside this task's scope.

route-tree.gen.ts is committed as a hand-written scaffold. It matches the structure TanStack Router codegen produces but does not include all extended metadata fields (e.g. routeMeta, loaderDeps). The Vite plugin will overwrite it with the fully-resolved output on first dev server start or build run.

eslint.config.ts stub exists alongside eslint.config.js. ESLint 9 uses the .js file by default; the .ts stub is excluded from tsconfig include and is inert. This is a minor untidiness that could be cleaned up if git file deletion becomes available.
typescript-eslint projectService mode requires a valid tsconfig to be reachable from the ESLint config location — this should work since tsconfig.json is in the same directory as eslint.config.js.
